conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
A bit off. I get tired easily, and sometimes my concentration drifts. But I have no idea what is causing it.
How long have you been feeling this tiredness and lack of focus?
Maybe a month or so? It started when I finished uni and didn't have as much structure in my days.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, definitely. I sit down to work on a project and I end up scrolling through social media for an hour.
How often does that happen when you're trying to get something done?
Pretty often. I try to tell myself it's just a phase, but it's annoying when I know I should be doing something else.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Bad. I wake up in the middle of the night and can't get back to sleep. Then I just lie there for ages.
How long have you been waking up like this?
Since the summer probably. I don't know why, maybe it's just the heat.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. Just watching TV and hanging out with mates. Nothing too exciting.
What kind of shows have you been watching?
Just whatever is on Netflix. I've been through a lot of documentaries recently.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
More of the same, probably. I'll probably just be trying to find a job or something.
Does "more of the same" feel pretty heavy to you?
A bit. It feels like I'm just drifting, but I don't know what else to do right now.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yeah, a bit. I just sort of sit there and wonder what to do.
How long do you usually find yourself sitting there, wondering?
Just a few minutes. Then I usually just decide on something, but it's annoying.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No. I'm always tired. I just try to get through it.
How long has it been since you've felt this way?
Since I finished uni. I guess it's just adjusting to not having a set routine anymore.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Not as much. I used to love reading, but now I just don't have the time.
What kind of books did you used to love reading?
History mostly. I don't know why I stopped.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just the fact that I'm not doing anything I went to uni for. It feels like a waste.
What would you like to be doing differently?
Working, probably. Just something that uses my degree.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
A bit frustrated. I feel like I'm just drifting and I don't know how to get back on track.
What does being "back on track" look like for you?
Just having a job, you know? Something to do with my degree.
How's your appetite been?
Okay, I guess. I eat when I'm hungry, but I'm not really thinking about food all the time.
Have you noticed any change in how often you feel hungry?
No, not really. I just eat when I need to.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
A bit. I get annoyed with small things, but I try to just calm down.
What are some of the small things that tend to annoy you?
Just people not listening or being slow. Stuff like that.
Is it more 'heavy and slow' or 'drained after little effort'?
I think it's more the latter. I just get annoyed easily, then I realize it's nothing.
When you realize it's nothing, what happens to your energy then?
It goes back up, mostly. I just take a deep breath and move on.
Does rest actually help, or are you still flat afterward?
Rest helps a bit, but I'm still tired. I guess it's just the way things are right now.
How long has this been the way things are for you?
Since I finished uni, probably.
Is the problem falling asleep, staying asleep, waking too early, or sleeping too much?
Waking up in the middle of the night.
How often does that happen?
Most nights, I guess.
How many nights a week is it like that?
I don't know, five or six.
Can you tell me more about what those nights are like for you?
Just lying there, awake. I try to go back to sleep, but it takes a while.
Even after sleep, do you wake up already tired?
Yeah, pretty much. I wake up tired and stay tired.
How long has that tired feeling lasted into your day?
All day, usually.
Does fatigue hit you in waves or stay steady all day?
It's steady, mostly.
When you say "mostly," are there times when it feels a bit different?
Yeah, when I'm doing something I like, I can push through the tiredness a bit.
When you picture the future, does anything feel workable, or mostly closed off?
Mostly closed off.
